annotation_target.structure: Second theme in G major — lighter, playful character
key_moments: m13 G major second theme enters
m16 dominant D in G major — open
teaching_goals: new theme character: lighter and more playful than mm. 1–4
adjust touch for G major brightness
style_tips: the second theme is an answer to the first — lighter

teaching_notes.note: Different character from m.1 — lighter, more conversational.
technique: slightly lighter arm weight than opening theme

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 13–16 of Piano Sonata No. 16 in C Major, K. 545, I. Allegro (continuation of mm. 9–12), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 13–16 and describe the second theme in G major.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 13–16.

Measures 13–16 arrive in G major with the second theme — a new melodic character. The melody is lighter and more playful than the opening, with a decorated top voice. The Alberti bass continues but now in G. Let me loop mm. 13–16.
Mozart K545 mm. 13–16: second theme in G major, lighter/more playful character. Pair with mm. 9–12 transition for key-arrival prediction.
